Markets anticipate recovery but underestimate the magnitude of cyclical earnings rebound.
causal_chain: FRED T10Y2Y crosses above 0 after 12+ months below zero
Un-inversion signals Fed easing cycle underway and recession troughing
Short rates falling faster than long rates = economic recovery expectations building
Cyclical sectors (XLI, XLF) see earnings troughs before defensives feel late-cycle pain
Long cyclicals (XLI+XLF) vs short defensives (XLP+XLU) captures the rotation over 12 months
data_sources: yfinance XLI, XLF, XLP, XLU, SPY
source_reference: FRED yield curve; Campbell Harvey dissertation (1986); recession-rotation timing literature

name: FRED ISM Services PMI Expansion Cross -> Long Consumer Discretionary (XLY)
thesis: FRED NMFBAI (ISM Non-Manufacturing/Services PMI) crossing above 55 after being below 50 for 3+ months signals a strong services sector recovery. Since the US is 70%+ services, this is a direct consumer demand signal. Long XLY for 6 months. ISM Services gets less attention than Manufacturing PMI but is far more relevant to consumer-facing equities. The expansion cross leads retail/hospitality earnings beats by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED NMFBAI (ISM Services PMI) crosses above 55 after 3+ months below 50
Services sector (70% of GDP) entering expansion = consumer demand recovering
Restaurant, hotel, travel, entertainment spending accelerates
Consumer discretionary companies (AMZN, HD, MCD, SBUX in XLY) see revenue recovery
XLY re-rates over 3-6 months as services-led consumer recovery flows to earnings
source_reference: ISM Non-Manufacturing Index; services-sector leading indicator research

name: FRED GDP Nowcast vs Consensus Gap -> Sector Surprise Trade
thesis: Atlanta Fed GDPNow (FRED GDPNOW, updated biweekly) frequently diverges from Blue Chip consensus by >1.5pp. When GDPNow exceeds consensus by >1.5pp, the economy is stronger than the market expects. Long SPY for 60 days. GDPNow is mechanical (model-driven) while consensus is behavioral (anchoring bias). The model beats humans 60%+ of the time at nowcasting. The divergence closes at GDP release.
causal_chain: FRED GDPNOW exceeds Blue Chip consensus GDP estimate by >1.5pp
Mechanical model incorporating latest data releases updating faster than humans
Positive surprise indicates economic momentum underpriced by consensus
Corporate earnings likely to beat as economy stronger than expected
SPY rallies 3-5% over 60 days as data confirms the nowcast
source_reference: Atlanta Fed GDPNow methodology; Higgins 2014 Atlanta Fed working paper

name: FRED Private Residential Construction Spending Acceleration -> Long Cement/Concrete (EXP/SUM)
thesis: FRED PRRESCONS (private residential construction spending, monthly) captures actual dollars spent on housing construction. When 3-month annualized growth exceeds +15%, concrete/cement demand is surging because foundations and flatwork are the earliest construction phases. Long EXP (Eagle Materials, 50% cement/concrete) and SUM (Summit Materials, 100% aggregates/cement) for 6 months. Private resi construction spending leads cement shipments by 1 month because the dollars are committed when the pour is scheduled.
causal_chain: FRED PRRESCONS 3-month annualized growth exceeds +15%
Residential construction spending surge = active home construction accelerating
Foundation/flatwork pours happen in month 1-2 of construction = cement demand immediate
EXP (50% cement/wallboard) and SUM (aggregates/cement) see volume growth
Pricing power + volume leverage = margin expansion over 3-6 months
source_reference: Census Bureau construction spending (C30); EXP/SUM investor presentations on residential exposure

name: FRED Building Permits Northeast Surge -> Long USG/JELD (Building Products)
thesis: FRED PERMIT (building permits) has regional breakdowns. When Northeast permits surge >20% YoY while national is flat, it signals regional housing demand shift. Northeast builds require higher R-value insulation, triple-pane windows, and more interior finishing due to climate. Long building product companies with Northeast distribution: JELD (windows/doors), AZEK (decking/trim), BLDR (specialty distribution). Regional permit data is free on FRED but markets focus only on national totals.
causal_chain: FRED PERMITNE (Northeast building permits) rises >20% YoY while PERMIT national is flat
Regional demand surge creates local supply bottleneck in building products
Northeast code requirements = higher-spec products (better windows, insulation, finishes)
JELD, AZEK, BLDR see regional order books fill with premium-spec products
Stocks re-rate as quarterly earnings show regional strength not in national consensus
source_reference: Census Bureau regional permits; Northeast building code requirements; JELD regional revenue mix

name: FRED JOLTS Quits Rate Recovery -> Long Staffing Companies (RHI/HAYS)
thesis: FRED JTSQUR (JOLTS quits rate, monthly) is a leading indicator of labor market confidence. When the quits rate rises >0.3pp from a cyclical trough, it signals workers are confident enough to leave jobs for better ones -- creating demand for staffing/temp agencies to fill vacancies. Long RHI (Robert Half) and HAYS for 6 months. Quits rate recovery leads staffing revenue by 1-2 quarters because every quit creates a vacancy that needs filling.
causal_chain: FRED JTSQUR (quits rate) rises >0.3pp from trailing 6-month low
Workers regaining confidence to quit = labor market strengthening
Every voluntary quit creates a vacancy that employers need to fill quickly
Staffing firms (RHI, HAYS) see temp/perm placement demand surge
Staffing company revenue accelerates 1-2 quarters after quits rate turns up
source_reference: BLS JOLTS; quits rate as labor confidence indicator (Daly-Hobijn 2013); staffing cycle research

name: FRED Average Weekly Hours Manufacturing Recovery -> Long Cyclical Equities
thesis: FRED AWHMAN (average weekly hours, manufacturing, monthly) is a leading indicator because employers increase hours BEFORE hiring new workers. When AWHMAN crosses above 41.0 hours after being below 40.5 for 4+ months, it signals manufacturers are ramping production. Long XLI for 6 months. Hours precede payrolls by 1-3 months and precede INDPRO by 2-4 months. This is one of the 10 LEI components but is rarely traded directly.
causal_chain: FRED AWHMAN crosses above 41.0 after 4+ months below 40.5
Manufacturers increasing overtime hours = demand exceeding current workforce capacity
Hours increase 1-3 months before new hiring (cheaper to pay OT than onboard)
New hiring follows -> payrolls accelerate -> production ramps -> INDPRO rises
XLI/cyclicals re-rate as production and employment data confirm recovery
source_reference: BLS CES; Conference Board LEI (AWHMAN is component #1); labor market leading indicators

name: FRED Continued Claims Declining Streak -> Long Small Caps (IWM)
thesis: FRED CCSA (continued claims, weekly) tracks the stock of unemployed workers receiving benefits. When continued claims decline for 8+ consecutive weeks after a peak, it signals the labor market is absorbing the unemployed -- a powerful recovery signal. Long IWM for 6 months. Small caps are the most employment-sensitive equities because small businesses account for 50%+ of US employment. Declining continued claims = small businesses hiring again = small cap revenue recovery.
causal_chain: FRED CCSA (continued claims) declines for 8+ consecutive weeks from a cyclical peak
Sustained decline = labor market absorbing unemployed workers (not just claims expiration)
Small businesses (50%+ of employment) are rehiring = revenue recovery for small firms
Small-cap earnings most leveraged to employment recovery (high labor cost share)
IWM rallies 15-25% over 6 months as small-cap earnings inflect
source_reference: BLS continued claims; small-cap employment sensitivity; DOL weekly claims report

name: FRED TED Spread Normalization -> Long Regional Banks (KRE)
thesis: FRED TEDRATE (3-month LIBOR minus T-bill rate) measures interbank credit risk. When TED spread contracts below 30bps after being above 50bps for 3+ months, it signals interbank trust is restored and funding stress is easing. Long KRE (regional bank ETF) for 6 months. Regional banks are most sensitive to funding markets because they rely on wholesale funding more than money-center banks. TED normalization leads regional bank NIM recovery by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED TEDRATE contracts below 30bps after 3+ months above 50bps
Interbank lending normalization = funding stress easing, trust restored
Regional banks' wholesale funding costs decline -> NIM expands
Loan origination resumes as funding is available at reasonable cost
KRE rallies 15-25% over 6 months as NIM + loan growth compound
source_reference: Federal Reserve TED spread; interbank credit risk literature; regional bank funding models

name: FRED Excess Reserves Surge -> Long Risk Assets (SPY/QQQ)
thesis: FRED EXCSRESNW (excess reserves of depository institutions, weekly) tracks idle cash sitting at the Fed. When excess reserves surge >20% in a rolling 3-month period, it signals massive liquidity injection that will eventually flow into risk assets. Long SPY for 6 months. Excess reserves are the 'fuel tank' for risk assets -- banks eventually deploy excess reserves into lending and securities purchases. The 2020 excess reserves surge preceded the equity rally by 2-3 months.
causal_chain: FRED WRESBAL (reserve balances, weekly) surges >20% in rolling 3 months
Fed liquidity injection creating excess reserves at depository institutions
Banks deploy excess into securities purchases and lending over 2-4 months
Capital flows from bank balance sheets to risk assets (equities, credit)
SPY/QQQ rally as excess liquidity transmission channel activates
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.4.1 balance sheet; bank reserves -> risk asset transmission; Poszar 2019

name: FRED C&I Loan Growth Inflection -> Long Bank Stocks (XLF)
thesis: FRED BUSLOANS (C&I loans at commercial banks, weekly) directly measures business lending. When C&I loan growth YoY turns positive after 6+ months of contraction, it confirms the credit cycle trough -- businesses are borrowing again. Long XLF for 12 months. C&I loan growth leads bank NII by 2-3 quarters and leads bank stock performance by 1-2 quarters. Distinct from PL064 (TOTBKCR total credit) -- this is specifically C&I loans, a purer business lending signal.
causal_chain: FRED BUSLOANS (C&I loans) YoY growth turns positive after 6+ months negative
Business borrowing resuming = capex and working capital demand recovering
New C&I loans generate net interest income for banks over 3-5 year loan duration
NII growth leads bank earnings acceleration by 1-2 quarters
XLF re-rates as bank earnings inflect on the credit growth recovery
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.8; C&I loan growth as credit cycle indicator; distinct from PL064 total credit

name: FRED Trade-Weighted Dollar Decline -> Long US Industrial Exporters (HON/CAT/DE)
thesis: FRED DTWEXBGS (trade-weighted dollar, broad, daily) declining >5% from a 6-month peak signals US export competitiveness improving. Industrial exporters (HON 55% intl, CAT 50% intl, DE 40% intl) benefit from both FX translation effects AND volume gains as US goods become cheaper overseas. Long HON+CAT+DE for 6 months. The dollar move leads export volume by 1-2 quarters (J-curve effect). Distinct from PL046 (DXY spike -> EM reversal) -- this is dollar WEAKNESS -> US exporter strength.
causal_chain: FRED DTWEXBGS declines >5% from trailing 6-month peak
Weaker dollar = US manufactured goods cheaper for foreign buyers
US industrial exporters (HON, CAT, DE) see foreign order books fill
FX translation tailwind boosts reported earnings by 3-5% at 50% intl revenue
Volume + FX = double tailwind for earnings -> stocks re-rate over 3-6 months
source_reference: FRED trade-weighted dollar; J-curve export lag; industrial company geographic revenue exposure

name: FRED US Trade Deficit Widening -> Long Port/Logistics Equities
thesis: FRED BOPGSTB (goods trade balance, monthly) widening beyond -$80B/month signals surging import volumes -- the US is pulling in massive physical goods. This import surge creates demand for port operators, logistics, and warehousing. Long EXPD (freight forwarder), CHRW (3PL), and ZIM/MATX (container shipping) for 6 months. Widening trade deficit = more physical goods hitting US ports = logistics revenue acceleration.
causal_chain: FRED BOPGSTB (goods trade balance) widens beyond -$80B monthly
Surging imports = massive physical goods volumes hitting US ports
Port congestion rises, container throughput at peak, logistics pricing power increases
EXPD (freight forwarding), CHRW (logistics), MATX (Jones Act containers) see revenue surge
Logistics stocks re-rate on pricing power and volume growth over 3-6 months
source_reference: Census Bureau FT900 trade; port throughput data; trade deficit -> logistics demand chain

name: FRED WTI Crude Backwardation Steepening -> Long E&P Equities (XOP)
thesis: When WTI futures curve shifts into steep backwardation (front-month > 6-month by >$3/bbl), it signals physical market tightness -- inventories drawing and current demand exceeding supply. Long XOP (E&P ETF) for 90 days. Backwardation rewards E&P producers because they sell at elevated spot prices while the market's forward expectations (which drive consensus models) are lower. This creates systematic earnings beats for spot-exposed producers.
causal_chain: WTI front-month minus 6-month spread exceeds $3/bbl (steep backwardation)
Backwardation signals physical tightness: inventories drawing, supply < demand now
E&P producers sell at elevated spot prices -- higher than sell-side model assumptions
Models based on strip pricing underestimate E&P earnings in backwardated markets
XOP beats consensus for 1-2 quarters during sustained backwardation
source_reference: EIA crude oil futures; backwardation as physical market signal; S&P GSCI backwardation research

name: FRED Henry Hub Natural Gas Below Cash Cost -> Long NG Producers (EQT/AR/RRC)
thesis: FRED MHHNGSP (Henry Hub spot price, monthly). When Henry Hub falls below $2.50/MMBtu (cash cost breakeven for marginal Appalachian producers), supply curtailments begin within 2-3 months. EQT (largest US gas producer) and AR (Antero) voluntarily shut-in production to defend price. Long EQT/AR/RRC for 12 months from the sub-$2.50 crossing. Sub-cash-cost pricing is self-correcting: supply curtailments + steady demand = price recovery. The 2020 and 2023-24 sub-$2.50 episodes both preceded 50%+ price recoveries.
causal_chain: FRED MHHNGSP (Henry Hub monthly) drops below $2.50/MMBtu
Sub-cash-cost pricing triggers producer shut-ins and completion deferrals within 2-3 months
EQT, AR announce voluntary curtailments (they've done this in 2020, 2024)
Supply reduction + steady demand (power gen baseload) = price recovery
NG=F recovers to $3.50-4.50 over 6-12 months; EQT/AR re-rate on higher realizations
data_sources: yfinance EQT, AR, RRC, NG=F, SPY
source_reference: EIA Henry Hub; EQT/AR curtailment press releases; natural gas cash cost analysis

name: FRED US Crude Oil Production MoM Decline -> Long Crude Futures
thesis: FRED MCRFPUS1 (US crude production monthly). When US production declines MoM for 3+ consecutive months, it signals depletion/curtailment overwhelming new completions. Long CL=F for 6 months. US is the world's swing producer at 13M+ bpd; a sustained decline tightens global supply by 200-500Kbpd which is material to global balances. Distinct from PL030 (rig count -> production lag) -- this is actual PRODUCTION declining, not a leading indicator.
causal_chain: FRED MCRFPUS1 (US crude production) declines MoM for 3 consecutive months
Actual production dropping = depletion + curtailments exceeding new completions
US produces 13M+ bpd; 200-500Kbpd decline is 0.2-0.5% of global supply
Global oil balance tightens -> inventories draw -> spot price rises
CL=F rallies 10-20% over 6 months as supply tightness is confirmed
source_reference: EIA monthly crude production; distinct from PL030 (rig count leading indicator)

name: FRED Air Revenue Passenger Miles Recovery -> Long Airlines (JETS ETF)
thesis: FRED AIRRPMTSI (air revenue passenger miles, monthly, seasonally adjusted) is the most direct measure of passenger airline demand. When RPM YoY crosses above +5% after a period of <0% for 6+ months, it confirms demand recovery. Long JETS (airline ETF) for 6 months. RPMs lead airline revenue by 1 quarter because tickets are sold 1-3 months before travel. Airlines have high operating leverage -- a 5% RPM increase can drive 15-20% EBIT growth if capacity is disciplined.
causal_chain: FRED AIRRPMTSI (revenue passenger miles) YoY crosses above +5% after 6+ months negative
Air travel demand confirmed recovering -- passengers physically flying more
Airlines have fixed costs (aircraft leases, labor) -- revenue growth flows to EBIT with 3-4x leverage
Ticket pricing holds if capacity discipline maintained (DAL/UAL load factors above 85%)
JETS ETF rallies 15-25% over 6 months on operating leverage from demand recovery
source_reference: BTS T-100 domestic; DOT air traffic statistics; airline operating leverage models

name: FRED Railroad Intermodal Traffic YoY Turn -> Long Intermodal/Rail (UNP/CSX)
thesis: FRED RAILFRTINTERam (railroad intermodal traffic) specifically tracks containerized freight moving by rail -- a proxy for consumer goods imports arriving at ports and moving inland. When intermodal YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative, it signals consumer demand recovery for physical goods. Long UNP+CSX for 6 months. Intermodal is the highest-margin freight for Class I rails. Distinct from PL052 (total carloads -> industrial production); intermodal tracks CONSUMER goods, not industrial bulk.
causal_chain: FRED RAILFRTINTERAM intermodal traffic YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative
Intermodal = containerized consumer goods (electronics, apparel, furniture) moving inland
Consumer goods demand recovery confirmed by physical rail traffic
UNP/CSX see intermodal volume recovery -- highest-margin freight category
Rail stocks re-rate as intermodal revenue accelerates and pricing power returns
source_reference: AAR intermodal statistics; distinct from PL052 (total carloads); intermodal-consumer demand link

name: FRED Consumer Sentiment (UMich) Extreme Low Reversal -> Long Retail (XRT)
thesis: FRED UMCSENT (U of Michigan Consumer Sentiment, monthly) at extreme lows (<55, bottom decile since 1978) is a contrarian BUY signal for retail stocks. When sentiment is this depressed, consumer expectations are already at rock bottom -- any stabilization drives spending surprise. Long XRT (retail ETF) for 6 months when UMCSENT drops below 55 and then rises for 2 consecutive months (trough confirmation). The signal fired in mid-2022 (UMCSENT=50) and preceded a 35% XRT rally.
causal_chain: FRED UMCSENT drops below 55 then rises for 2 consecutive months (trough confirmed)
Extreme pessimism = consumer expectations at rock bottom, positioned for positive surprise
Consumers spending more than sentiment surveys imply (actions > words)
Retail revenue beats low expectations -> XRT earnings revisions turn positive
XRT rallies 15-30% over 6 months as sentiment normalization drives spending recovery
source_reference: U of Michigan Consumer Sentiment; contrarian sentiment trading literature; 2022 case study

name: FRED Personal Savings Rate Decline from Peak -> Long Leisure/Hospitality (PEJ)
thesis: FRED PSAVERT (personal savings rate, monthly). When savings rate declines >4pp from a cyclical peak (e.g., drops from 12% to 8%), it signals consumers are DIS-SAVING to fund spending. This spending surge benefits leisure/hospitality (restaurants, hotels, travel) most because these are the first discretionary categories consumers re-engage with. Long PEJ (leisure/hospitality ETF) or BKNG+MAR+H for 6 months. The 2021-2022 savings rate collapse (34% -> 3%) preceded the leisure spending boom.
causal_chain: FRED PSAVERT declines >4pp from trailing 12-month peak
Consumers drawing down savings to fund spending = demand acceleration
Leisure/hospitality (restaurants, hotels, travel) are first beneficiaries of dis-saving
BKNG, MAR, H, restaurant chains see RevPAR/same-store sales acceleration
Leisure stocks re-rate on earnings beats over 3-6 months
source_reference: BEA personal income/savings; consumer spending cycle; 2021-2022 revenge spending precedent

name: FRED Retail Sales ex-Auto ex-Gas MoM Beat -> Long Consumer Discretionary
thesis: FRED RSFSXMV (retail sales excluding motor vehicles) removes the volatile auto component. When MoM growth exceeds +0.5% for 2 consecutive months after 3+ months of flat/negative, it signals core consumer demand inflecting. Long XLY for 90 days. The retail sales report drops mid-month and moves markets same-day, but the FOLLOW-THROUGH over the next 60 days is under-traded because markets price the headline instantly but not the trend confirmation.
causal_chain: FRED RSFSXMV (retail sales ex-auto) MoM > +0.5% for 2 consecutive months
Core consumer spending inflecting positively after a soft patch
Retailers see same-store sales accelerate, especially discretionary categories
Next month's retail report likely confirms the trend (momentum in consumer data)
XLY rallies 3-5% over 60-90 days as spending trend confirmation flows through
source_reference: Census Bureau advance retail sales; retail sales ex-auto as cleaner demand signal

name: FRED Non-Defense Capital Goods Orders ex-Aircraft Inflection -> Long Capital Goods (ETN/ROK/AME)
thesis: FRED NEWORDER (core capital goods orders = non-defense ex-aircraft, monthly) is the purest measure of business investment demand. When 3-month MA turns positive YoY after 6+ months of decline, it signals the capex cycle recovery. Long ETN (Eaton, electrical equipment), ROK (Rockwell, factory automation), AME (AMETEK, electronic instruments) for 6 months. Core capex orders lead equipment-maker revenue by 2-3 quarters because orders become deliveries/revenue on a 6-9 month cycle.
causal_chain: FRED NEWORDER (core capex orders) 3-month MA turns positive YoY after 6+ months negative
Business investment inflecting = companies ordering new equipment, machinery, automation
Equipment makers (ETN, ROK, AME) see order backlogs rebuild
Revenue accelerates 2-3 quarters later as orders convert to deliveries
Capital goods stocks re-rate on improving order books and revenue visibility
source_reference: Census Bureau core capital goods orders; Conference Board LEI component; capex cycle timing

name: FRED Industrial Production Manufacturing Index Breakout -> Long Factory Automation (ISRG/FANUY/ROK)
thesis: FRED IPMAN (Industrial Production: Manufacturing, monthly) crossing above its 12-month MA after being below for 6+ months is a confirmed manufacturing recovery signal. Long factory automation plays (ROK Rockwell, FANUY Fanuc, TER Teradyne) for 6 months. When manufacturing recovers, the first investment is in automation to boost productivity before hiring. Automation companies see order growth 1-2 quarters ahead of broader industrials because automation capex precedes expansion hiring.
causal_chain: FRED IPMAN crosses above 12-month MA after 6+ months below
Manufacturing output recovery confirmed -- factories increasing production
First capex priority: automation/robotics to boost output without hiring (labor tight)
ROK (factory automation), FANUY (industrial robots), TER (test automation) see order surge
Automation stocks re-rate ahead of broader industrials by 1-2 quarters
source_reference: Federal Reserve IP; automation-first capex cycle pattern; IFR robot installation data

name: FRED 5Y Breakeven Inflation Drop Below 2% -> Long TIPS (TIP ETF)
thesis: FRED T5YIE (5-year breakeven inflation rate, daily) below 2.0% signals the market is pricing inflation below the Fed's target. When T5YIE drops below 2.0% after being above 2.3% for 6+ months, TIPS are undervalued relative to nominal bonds. Long TIP (TIPS ETF) for 12 months. At sub-2% breakevens, TIPS outperform nominals if inflation comes in at or above the Fed's 2% target (which it has 80%+ of the time since 2000). The breakeven below target is a systematic mis-pricing.
causal_chain: FRED T5YIE drops below 2.0% after 6+ months above 2.3%
Market pricing inflation below Fed target = deflationary expectations excessive
TIPS coupon adjusts for actual CPI; if CPI > breakeven, TIPS outperform nominals
Fed targets 2% inflation and has tools to deliver it (rate cuts, QE if needed)
TIP outperforms IEF (nominal intermediate) by 3-5% over 12 months as breakevens normalize
source_reference: FRED breakeven inflation; TIPS vs nominal relative value; Fleckenstein-Longstaff-Lustig 2014 JFE

name: FRED Real Fed Funds Rate Turning Negative -> Long Growth Equities (QQQ)
thesis: FRED DFF (fed funds rate) minus FRED PCEPILFE (core PCE YoY) = real fed funds rate. When real fed funds turns negative (Fed rate below inflation), monetary policy is accommodative and financial conditions are easy. Long QQQ for 12 months. Growth stocks (tech, biotech) are the highest-duration equities and benefit most from negative real rates because their DCF valuations are most sensitive to discount rates. Every period of negative real rates since 2000 has preceded growth outperformance.
causal_chain: Real fed funds rate (DFF - core PCE) turns negative
Monetary policy accommodative = borrowing cost below inflation = free money in real terms
Financial conditions ease -> credit available -> speculative investment rises
Growth/tech stocks (longest duration equities) re-rate most on lower real discount rates
QQQ outperforms SPY by 10-20% over 12 months during negative real rate periods
data_sources: FRED DFF (daily, free), FRED PCEPILFE (monthly, free)
source_reference: Taylor Rule; real rate regime and equity duration; negative real rate -> growth outperformance

name: FRED 10Y-3M Yield Curve Steepening After Inversion -> Long Duration Bonds (TLT)
thesis: FRED T10Y3M (10Y minus 3M spread) steepening from deeply inverted (below -100bps) back through zero signals the Fed cutting cycle is underway. Long TLT for 12 months from the zero-crossing. The 10Y-3M curve is the most reliable recession predictor (Estrella-Mishkin), and its UN-inversion signals the rate-cutting cycle. TLT rallies 15-25% during cutting cycles as long-end yields follow the front-end down with a lag.
causal_chain: FRED T10Y3M crosses above 0 after being below -100bps for 6+ months
Un-inversion = Fed cutting aggressively, front-end yields falling fast
Long-end yields follow with a lag but also decline as recession fears ease
TLT price (inverse of 20Y+ yields) rallies on both rate expectations and duration bid
TLT returns 15-25% over 12 months during a typical Fed cutting cycle
source_reference: Estrella-Mishkin 1998; FRED yield curve; cutting cycle -> duration rally pattern
